evidence: Phases 1-4 are pure validation gates: they check existence/quality of artifacts produced by external contracts and have NO invocation of any generation script when artifacts are absent.
Phase 3 failure required external bootstrap script (bootstrap_fastapi_40x.py) outside the orchestrator's governance boundary.
Phase 4 is currently blocked (GAP-REG-02) and would require another external bootstrap script to resolve.
Phase 5 requires dom_layer_path and ceu_grounding_registry.json as pre-existing inputs — no prior phase generates these.
Phase 8a requires dom_layer_path and integration_validation_path — both fields missing from FastAPI source_manifest.json.

The orchestrator cannot produce a complete client run from registered source alone: it generates only final outputs (binding envelope, signals, vault, reports, selector) not the structural and grounding preconditions.

alternatives_evaluated.reason: A true orchestrator owns the full artifact lifecycle from registered source to final outputs. This orchestrator does not invoke any script to generate Phases 1-4 artifacts when absent.
Phases 1-4 are pure validation gates. But Phases 5-9 do generate artifacts. The pipeline is not purely a gate.

A hybrid orchestrator would consciously route between validation and generation based on artifact state. This pipeline always validates in Phases 1-4 and has no conditional generation path when validation fails.

The pipeline has the structure and intent of a full orchestrator but is missing generation capability for the prerequisite stages (1-4). It is incomplete in the specific sense: the generation half is missing, not the validation half.

remediation_rationale.description: Extend run_client_pipeline.py (or a new orchestrator that invokes it) to own the full artifact lifecycle. When Phase N's required artifact is absent, the orchestrator should invoke the appropriate generation script for that phase rather than fail-closing with an external contract reference. This is the only remediation that resolves the systemic drift risk.
scope: Phase 1-2: Integrate intake extraction as a callable phase (or add pre-pipeline intake orchestration)
Phase 3: Integrate bootstrap_fastapi_40x.py (or a generic structural scanner) as a callable sub-phase
Phase 4: Integrate grounding state migration/generation as a callable sub-phase
Phase 5: Ensure all required input fields (dom_layer_path, ceu_grounding_registry) are either generated by an upstream phase or resolvable from a well-defined conformance path
Phase 8a: Ensure dom_layer_path and integration_validation_path are resolvable for all registered clients
Registration contract: Update source_manifest schema validation to catch missing required fields at registration time

alternatives_rejected.reason: Phases 5-9 genuinely generate artifacts. Renaming the pipeline as a validator would misrepresent its generation capability and require a separate orchestration layer to be built from scratch.

This is the current de facto state — each phase failure triggers an external bootstrap. Formalizing it as a 'preflight contract' pattern does not resolve the drift risk (O(clients × phases) external scripts) and codifies non-repeatable onboarding.

The canonical E2E contract is internally consistent for BlueEdge. The inconsistency is in the multi-client generalization: the contract was established for a client whose precondition artifacts already existed. It does not define new client bootstrapping. This is a gap, not an inconsistency.

conformance_path_model_note: An alternative architectural decision is available: formally declare the pipeline a 'conformance-path orchestrator' where all pre-Phase-5 artifacts are provided via a client-specific conformance artifact set (analogous to fastapi_conformance_path). This would require: (1) defining a formal client conformance artifact schema, (2) updating registration contracts to require conformance artifact production, (3) removing or clearly scoping the validation-only phases. This is a valid design but must be explicitly declared, not arrived at by default.
blocking_before_next_stream: If UPGRADE: define which phases become generation phases and which sub-scripts they invoke
If CONFORMANCE_PATH: define the full conformance artifact schema for FastAPI and produce conformance artifacts for all phases
